issues.title: Missing Error Handling in Ingestion Command
issues.details: The ingestion command lacks comprehensive error handling, particularly in the upsert phase. This could lead to unhandled exceptions and data loss. Suggested fix: Implement try-catch blocks around critical operations and ensure that errors are logged and handled gracefully. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:3-5.

issues.title: Potential Path Traversal Vulnerability
issues.details: The ingestion command allows user-defined paths without validation, which could lead to path traversal attacks. Suggested fix: Validate and sanitize input paths to ensure they do not escape the intended directory structure. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:1-2.

issues.title: Lack of Unit Tests for New Logic
issues.details: The new ingestion logic lacks unit tests, which are essential for ensuring correctness and preventing regressions. Suggested fix: Write unit tests covering various scenarios for the ingestion command. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md.

issues.title: Inconsistent Commit Message Format
issues.details: The commit message format is not consistently followed across commands, which can lead to confusion. Suggested fix: Enforce a standardized commit message format across all commands. File: .claude/commands/features.md.

summary: The overall code quality shows several areas for improvement, particularly in error handling, security, and testing. Addressing these issues is crucial for maintaining a robust and secure application.
